Revenue.title: Revenue
Revenue.description: Represents a single revenue stream for the business.

id.description: Unique identifier for the revenue entry.

Expense.title: Expense
Expense.description: Represents a single expense incurred by the business.

id.description: Unique identifier for the expense entry.

category.description: Category of the expense (e.g., supplies, rent, utilities).

startTime.description: The starting date and time of the appointment.

endTime.description: The ending date and time of the appointment.

attendees.description: Attendees of the appointment. Can be a list of names or references to a User/Contact entity.

Vehicle.title: Vehicle
Vehicle.description: Represents a single vehicle in the fleet.

kenteken.description: The license plate of the vehicle, used as the unique identifier.

merk.description: The make of the vehicle (e.g., VOLKSWAGEN).

model.description: The model of the vehicle (e.g., CADDY).

bouwjaar.description: The year the vehicle was manufactured.

brandstof.description: The type of fuel the vehicle uses.

apk_vervaldatum.description: The expiration date of the APK.

status.description: Current status of the vehicle (e.g., Actief).

definition.description: Collection to store user profiles. (Although the user entity wasn't explicitly defined, this path is necessary for user-specific data.)

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Collection to store revenue entries for a specific user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: revenueId
params.description: The unique identifier of the revenue entry.

definition.description: Collection to store expense entries for a specific user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: expenseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the expense entry.

definition.description: Collection to store appointment entries for a specific user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: appointmentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the appointment entry.

definition.description: Collection to store vehicle entries for a specific user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: vehicleId
params.description: The unique identifier of the vehicle entry (license plate).

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore data structure is designed to support a basic ERP system for small businesses, focusing on revenue, expenses, and appointments.  User data is segregated under `/users/{userId}` for private data. Revenue, Expenses, and Appointments are stored as subcollections under each user's document to ensure data isolation and ownership. A `vehicles` collection is added to store fleet information per user. This structure facilitates simple security rules based on the authenticated user's ID. The structure enforces Authorization Independence. No `get()` operations are required in rules. Each collection under `/users/{userId}` is effectively private to that user, enabling straightforward read/write rules based on `request.auth.uid`. QAPs are supported because list operations will be scoped to the user's own data, ensuring that users can only access their own revenue, expenses, appointments, and vehicles.
